title: Pruning Risk Model — Three Options Compared

option_a_keep_all.description: Keep all 654 technically clean URLs in sitemap. No pruning.
cons: 350 thin pages dilute sitemap quality signal
Google may perceive site as low-quality
Crawl budget diluted across thin pages
option_a_keep_all.risk: LOW — status quo. But DNI stagnation may continue.

option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.description: Remove from sitemap: all 125 guides + 58 tools with 0 impressions in 28 days. Keep 8 tools with GSC impressions. Sitemap reduces to ~471 URLs.
pros: Crawl budget focuses on proven pages
Pages remain live for users who navigate to them
Reversible — just add back to sitemap
cons: 58 removed tool pages might have future GSC potential
Guides provide some user navigation value
option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.risk: LOW-MEDIUM — removing from sitemap doesn't delete pages. Users can still find them via /all-pages navigation.
option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.indexing_trust_impact: POSITIVE — cleaner sitemap signals quality focus
option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.crawl_demand_impact: POSITIVE — Google crawls fewer thin pages, more budget for high-value pages
option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.traffic_loss_risk: VERY LOW — removed URLs have 0 clicks and <10 total impressions
option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.implementation_risk: LOW — add guide and thin-tool lists to sitemap exclusion filter
option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.rollback_difficulty: EASY — remove exclusion lists, re-add to sitemap
option_b_remove_zero_impression_thin.recommendation: RECOMMENDED — best balance of quality improvement and risk minimization

option_c_noindex_consolidate_delete.description: Aggressive: noindex guides, consolidate thin tools into parent calculators via 301 redirects, delete dead pages.
pros: Eliminates all thin content from Google's view
cons: High implementation risk — 301 redirects need careful mapping
User experience impact if guides are useful to some visitors
Overkill for the current problem — technical sitemap is already clean
option_c_noindex_consolidate_delete.risk: HIGH — bulk noindex/redirect/delete can cause unintended consequences
option_c_noindex_consolidate_delete.rollback_difficulty: DIFFICULT — 301s and deletions are hard to reverse
option_c_noindex_consolidate_delete.recommendation: NOT RECOMMENDED — too aggressive for the current situation. Sitemap is already technically clean. Option B achieves 90% of the benefit with 10% of the risk.

recommendation: Option B — Remove 183 thin URLs (125 guides + 58 zero-impression tools) from sitemap. Keep 8 tools with GSC impressions. Execute in Phase 32.5 after approval.
